target_scopes: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker
Critical reward extraction or unfair reward access by an unprivileged attacker
live_context_hint: Use ACTE's block-pinned target, balances, dependencies, ABI, events, views, mapping keys, and failed probes.
protocol_focus: Audit the current Torus deployment on ethereum for concrete reachable extraction.
attacker_profile: unprivileged external caller, token holder, depositor, trader, claimant, receiver/callback contract, or flash-loan user
core_invariants: No unprivileged caller receives protocol or user assets beyond valid entitlement.
On-chain balances and internal liabilities reconcile across every asset-moving path.
Repeated, reordered, callback, rounding, and boundary executions cannot create extractable value.
Privileged configuration is not an exploit unless an unprivileged path bypasses it.
impact_mapping: Fund extraction requires attacker-controlled value gain at protocol or user expense.
Reward extraction requires overclaim, replay, eligibility bypass, or unfair entitlement increase.
Accounting corruption counts only when connected to a reachable extraction transaction.
known_rejection_memory: Reject DoS, griefing, gas, liveness, admin compromise, and source-only theories without attacker value gain.
Reject claims that do not match the current target address, bytecode, and captured live state.
Reject critical claims without a reproducible local or pinned-fork proof plan.
